author: Dr. Lizard
title: What is the clinical significance of C3d positivity on direct antiglobulin testing in PNH?
answer: C3d positivity confirms C3 fragment opsonization causing extravascular hemolysis and indicates need for proximal complement inhibition.
detail: C3d-coated PNH erythrocytes are recognized by C3-specific receptors on macrophages in liver and spleen.
This finding distinguishes complement-mediated extravascular hemolysis from other causes of persistent anemia on C5 inhibitors.
Positive C3d direct antiglobulin test supports switching from terminal to proximal complement blockade therapy.
releaseNote: C3d positivity on direct antiglobulin testing in PNH indicates C3 fragment opsonization and extravascular hemolysis, requiring proximal complement inhibition therapy.
